name: Microsoft Fara and local small computer-use models
maintenance_status: Active Microsoft Research project. The repository had material May-June 2026 updates for Fara1.5 notes, WebTailBench V2, verifier benchmarks, critical-point-aware verification, and retry termination. Fara-7B weights and harness are released. Fara1.5 4B/9B/27B results were announced on 2026-05-21, but the public Fara repository still said its agent harness was coming soon and no official Fara1.5 Hugging Face weights were found on 2026-07-09 [uncertain].
license: Fara-7B code and weights are MIT; its Qwen2.5-VL dependency is Apache-2.0. Both are compatible with ApplyPilot's AGPL-3.0 codebase when notices and attribution are preserved. Fara1.5 weight and harness licensing is [uncertain] until those artifacts are publicly released with terms.
pricing_model: Open-weight self-hosting: no per-token license charge for released Fara-7B. Cost is owned or rented GPU/CPU/NPU hardware, electricity, model serving, browser compute, storage, and engineering. Azure AI Foundry or other hosted endpoints, if selected, use their own pricing. Fara1.5 commercial availability and pricing are [uncertain].
marginal_cost_per_attempt: [uncertain] On already-owned hardware, incremental cash cost can approach electricity plus wear: roughly $0.005-$0.10 for a several-minute browser/model attempt depending on GPU power, duration, utilization, and quantization. Fully loaded GPU amortization or cloud rental can raise this materially. Benchmark joules, wall time, and allocated hardware dollars rather than assuming zero model cost.
projected_cost_per_verified_apply: [uncertain] A hypothetical $0.01-$0.10 local attempt at ApplyPilot historical rates yields $0.03-$0.30 per Ashby verified apply at 33.7%, $0.03-$0.35 for Greenhouse at 28.6%, $0.12-$1.20 for other hosts at 8.3%, and $1-$10 for Workday at 1.0%, before hardware amortization, fallback, or labor. Fara-7B's 28.0% WebTailBench Jobs/Careers result is not a submit-confirmed ApplyPilot rate.
billing_granularity: Owned hardware accrues power and amortization by elapsed use and reservation; cloud GPUs bill by provider time unit; local serving has idle power and model-load costs. The open weights have no request minimum. [uncertain] Exact cost depends on the target GPU/NPU, quantization, batch/concurrency, and whether idle capacity is treated as sunk or allocated.
model_cost: [uncertain] Released Fara-7B has no token fee when self-hosted. FP16/BF16 weights alone are roughly 14 GB before KV cache and runtime overhead; the model card hints tensor parallel size 2 may be needed when memory is insufficient. Quantized community variants and ONNX assets can lower memory, but ApplyPilot quality and throughput must be measured. Fara1.5 model artifacts are not yet confirmed public.
infrastructure_cost: [uncertain] Requires a model server such as vLLM/SGLang or supported ONNX runtime, GPU/NPU memory, host RAM, storage for weights and screenshots, Playwright Chromium, and power/cooling. One 7B model may fit a capable consumer GPU with quantization, while 27B-class models need substantially more memory. Hardware floor and throughput are deployment-specific.
human_recovery_cost: [uncertain] Fara is trained to stop at critical points including signing in, entering personal data, sending communications, and submitting job applications, so human or deterministic recovery is part of the intended safety model. At $50/hour, five minutes costs $4.17; even a small intervention rate can dominate local inference cost.
adapter_maintenance_tco: [uncertain] Planning estimate: 4-8 engineer-weeks to productionize the experimental harness, local serving, Windows/Linux GPU support, profile/OTP handoff, restricted action tools, deterministic submit/verification, crash supervision, telemetry, and ATS benchmarks; then 12-24 hours/month for model/runtime/browser/site drift. Local cost savings must amortize this work.
verified_completion_rate: [uncertain] Fara-7B reports 73.5% WebVoyager, 34.1% Online-Mind2Web, 38.4% WebTailBench overall, and 28.0% on its 50 Jobs/Careers tasks, averaged over three runs. Fara1.5 reports 80.8/86.6/88.6 WebVoyager and 57.3/63.4/72.0 Online-Mind2Web for 4B/9B/27B. None is an ATS-stratified, positively confirmed job-application rate, and Fara's critical-point policy stops before submit.
failure_modes: Coordinate grounding error, stale screenshot, planning loop failure, local model hallucination, critical-point stop, verifier error, browser/CDP crash, model-server OOM or timeout, GPU driver/runtime incompatibility, profile lock, popup/tab confusion, auth/OTP/challenge, unsupported fields, upload/iframe failure, prompt injection, and false success without network/DOM confirmation.
crash_recovery: The open harness emits action histories and evaluation trajectories, and the repository includes verifier/error-taxonomy work, retry caps, and Playwright automation. There is no documented transactional resume or exactly-once submit recovery. Save pre-submit state and artifacts, restart only before submit, and quarantine any crash after an irreversible action.
exactly_once_submit_safety: Fara's critical-point training is a useful stop boundary: job application submission should require permission or sensitive data. It is not third-party transaction idempotency. Keep submit outside the model action set and expose a deterministic one-time ApplyPilot tool with lease, dedup key, plan hash, policy approval, response capture, and ambiguous-result quarantine.
verification_evidence_tier: The harness and CUAVerifierBench support screenshot/action-trajectory verification, and direct Playwright code can capture DOM/network evidence. For production, require ATS/application ID or successful submit response first, explicit confirmation DOM second, delayed email corroboration third; model/verifier judgment or screenshot alone must not mark applied.
verification_latency: [uncertain] Local inference avoids remote model latency but may be slower per step on modest hardware. Deterministic network/DOM verification can run synchronously; local multimodal verifier passes add compute, and email is delayed. Measure model, browser, verification, and reconciliation phases by hardware and ATS.
headless_headed_parity: [uncertain] The harness uses Playwright screenshots and can run local browsers, while Magentic-UI provides human-in-the-loop interaction. Rendering, viewport, font, challenge, and GPU screenshot-pipeline differences can change coordinate grounding. No ApplyPilot headed/headless parity benchmark exists.
python_integration: Strong at the language boundary. The Fara repository installs as a Python package, exposes fara-cli, uses Playwright, and serves models through OpenAI-compatible vLLM/SGLang endpoints. ApplyPilot can integrate in Python, but model serving and the experimental harness should run as supervised processes with pinned versions.
playwright_cdp_reuse: [uncertain] Fara's public harness installs and controls Playwright, but primary documentation does not establish safe attachment to ApplyPilot's existing Chrome profiles/CDP ports as a supported contract. A wrapper can likely use Playwright/CDP directly, but profile ownership, tab selection, and controller exclusivity need explicit implementation and tests.
migration_effort: [uncertain] Medium to large. Python compatibility helps, but the route needs GPU/model deployment, action API restrictions, profile and critical-point UI, answer-plan/provenance binding, deterministic submit/verification, evidence joins, and ATS-specific canaries. Fara1.5 cannot be a dependency until public artifacts and license are confirmed.
ats_fit: [uncertain] Best viewed as an experimental owned-hardware floor for unusual long-tail pages and read-only/pre-submit assistance. Fara-7B's Jobs/Careers benchmark is only 28.0% and does not include safe confirmed submission. Stable ATSs remain adapter-first; Workday should be excluded until a much stronger targeted result exists.
deterministic_coverage: The browser harness can combine direct Playwright operations with local model actions, and final submit should be deterministic. [uncertain] The share completed without model inference and the share of repeated signatures promotable to adapters are unknown. Local inference being cheap does not justify replacing stable deterministic paths.
escape_rate: [uncertain] Expected escapes include Fara critical-point stops, grounding/planning failure, OOM/time limit, unsupported fields, auth/OTP/challenge, missing provenance, and failed positive verification. Measure model-to-human and model-to-premium fallback before submit; after submit, only ambiguity reconciliation is permitted.
control_plane_overhead: [uncertain] Each step sends a screenshot, task, and textual action/thought history to the local model and returns coordinate-based actions. Overhead includes screenshot capture/encoding, model prefill/decode, browser action, history growth, and verifier passes. Record pixels/bytes, prompt/output tokens, GPU memory/utilization, joules, actions, retries, and wall time.
observability_joinability: Moderate to strong if built into the wrapper. The open harness produces trajectories and action history; local model servers expose request metrics; Playwright can record network, DOM, screenshots, video, and traces. Attach ApplyPilot attempt_id to every model request and artifact and persist model hash, quantization, hardware, critical-point state, evidence IDs, and energy/cost.
persistent_profiles: [uncertain] Local ownership permits dedicated persistent Chrome profiles and strict on-machine storage, but Fara documentation does not provide profile locking, cloning, migration, or cleanup semantics. ApplyPilot must own one profile/account lock, browser-version pinning, encrypted storage, and clean shutdown.
existing_session_attach: [uncertain] Local deployment makes CDP attachment technically feasible through Playwright, but the released Fara harness does not document arbitrary existing-session attachment as a supported feature. Prototype against a dedicated ApplyPilot CDP browser and prohibit concurrent controllers.
login_and_otp: Fara's model card explicitly treats signing in, entering personal information, and submitting applications as critical points where the agent should stop. Magentic-UI offers a human-in-the-loop experience. ApplyPilot should collect OTP/sensitive values outside model context, use a deterministic fill step, obtain permission, and verify login before resuming.
extension_support: [uncertain] Local Playwright/Chromium can be launched with reviewed extensions or attach to a dedicated profile, but Fara does not document extension compatibility, MV3 service workers, native messaging, or secret isolation. Keep extensions narrowly permissioned and outside model-controlled navigation until tested.
session_data_boundary: Self-hosted Fara can keep screenshots, cookies, resumes, prompts, traces, and model inference on owned hardware, which is its main privacy advantage. Downloads of weights/code and optional telemetry/provider services still have their own boundaries. Restrict model-server network access, encrypt profiles/artifacts, and prevent page content from invoking filesystem or shell capabilities.
required_field_completeness: Screenshot reasoning can notice rendered controls, but cannot guarantee all off-screen, conditional, accessibility-only, or iframe required fields. Combine it with deterministic DOM/accessibility and browser-validity inventories before planning and again before submit; stop on unknown required controls.
answer_provenance: Fara predicts actions from task, screenshots, and history; it does not inherently constrain form answers to approved evidence. Supply a signed ApplyPilot answer plan and deterministic lookup/fill tools with source IDs, and forbid the model from generating personal, demographic, work-authorization, compensation, or employment claims.
unsupported_question_fallback: Use Fara's critical-point behavior plus an ApplyPilot classifier to stop for sensitive, unknown, legal, demographic, compensation, work-authorization, communication, and submit decisions. Return needs_review to an owner or deterministic policy engine; never reinterpret a stop as permission to guess.
prompt_injection_resistance: [uncertain] The Fara-7B model card reports safety post-training, critical-point recognition, red teaming, sandbox recommendations, and internet allow/block lists, but no ATS hostile-page success rate or secret-exfiltration guarantee. Run in a sandbox with strict domains, no shell/filesystem tool, isolated secrets, and malicious screenshot/text fixtures.
site_permission_and_terms: [uncertain] Open weights and an MIT license do not authorize automation on employer or ATS sites. Review each interface and account policy, use user-authorized flows, respect rate/access controls, and do not use local models or browser infrastructure to evade challenges. Microsoft's model guidance also advises caution for commercial and high-stakes use.
irreversible_action_guard: Fara's documented critical points include submitting job applications, making it well aligned with a hard permission boundary. Enforce that boundary in code by removing generic submit capability and requiring a deterministic one-time tool after lease, provenance, completeness, dedup, host-policy, and fresh-page checks. Begin with no-submit canaries.
concurrency: [uncertain] Open software imposes no account cap, but practical concurrency is limited by GPU memory/throughput, model batching, Playwright/browser RAM, profile locks, and verifier load. A single 7B server may batch multiple sessions, but latency and grounding quality by batch size must be measured; 27B-class models need more hardware.
startup_latency: [uncertain] Cold startup includes loading multi-gigabyte weights and launching Playwright; warm requests avoid model load. Fara-7B may require one or two GPUs depending on memory and runtime. Measure service load, browser/CDP connection, first-token, first-action, and warm-step latency on each target machine.
warm_session_reuse: Local model servers and Playwright browsers can remain warm across attempts, reducing load time. Reuse only with exclusive profile/account locks, clean-tab and download reset, model/request state reset, health checks, idle timeout, and artifact separation to avoid leaking one application into another.
tracing_and_replay: The repository includes evaluation trajectories, action histories, screenshots, verifier pipelines, and error taxonomy; Playwright can add trace/HAR/video. These support audit and offline scoring but not deterministic action replay or mid-submit resume. Promote stable form signatures into ApplyPilot adapters rather than replaying coordinate actions blindly.
deployment_modes: Local/self-hosted Python harness with vLLM or SGLang, Docker-capable model serving, ONNX/NPU assets for released Fara-7B, Magentic-UI integration, owned GPU/NPU workstation or server, cloud GPU VM, and hybrid local model plus dedicated Playwright browser. Fara1.5 deployment artifacts remain [uncertain].
browser_version_matrix: [uncertain] The released harness installs Playwright and centers on Chromium/browser screenshots. Firefox, WebKit, Safari, and WebDriver BiDi compatibility are not documented. Pin model, processor, harness, Playwright, Chromium, viewport, fonts, and quantization, then rerun ATS fixtures after any change.
canary_stop_loss: Use sandbox/domain allowlists, a hard critical-point stop, maximum steps/tokens/wall time/energy, GPU OOM supervision, one pre-submit retry, no post-submit retry, and zero tolerance for duplicate submit, provenance violations, or secret exposure. Quarantine missing confirmation and trip route-level breakers by ATS/model/hardware.
benchmark_design: Compare deterministic adapters, current premium fallback, Fara-7B, and any officially released Fara1.5 small model on matched ATS strata. Include repeated synthetic forms with conditional controls, visual-only widgets, uploads, iframes, prompt injection, auth, critical points, OOM/crash, and confirmation; use unique live jobs and positive evidence.
route_funnel: Track eligibility, local model ready, browser/profile acquired, auth valid, deterministic inventory, Fara selected, steps/tokens/joules, critical point triggered, plan/provenance complete, permission/submit guard approved, deterministic submit called, network/DOM evidence, fallback before submit, human recovery, ambiguous quarantine, email reconciliation, and final disposition.
step_context_budget: [uncertain] Initial Fara-7B canary: at most 20 model steps, 1280x720 screenshots, five minutes, one pre-submit retry, no model access to generic submit, and a measured energy/hardware budget equivalent to $0.10 before fallback. Limit history and verifier passes based on measured quality and GPU memory.
cache_replay_yield: [uncertain] Local serving can reuse model weights and KV/prefix state, but this is not safe no-model browser replay. Measure repeated ATS form signatures and convert stable sequences to deterministic Playwright adapters keyed by tenant, locale, form and answer schema. Report warm-model savings separately from adapter hit rate.
confidence_intervals: [uncertain] Microsoft reports benchmark scores averaged over three runs, but no ApplyPilot interval exists. Report Wilson 95% intervals for positive completion, critical-point/human rate, fallback, OOM, and ambiguity by ATS/model/hardware; bootstrap fully loaded cost, energy, and latency per verified apply.
historical_bucket_impact: [uncertain] A local model could lower marginal cost for the agent/browser-runtime long tail and preserve data locally, but Fara-7B's 28.0% Jobs/Careers benchmark and mandatory critical-point behavior suggest substantial fallback. It does not address preflight/policy failures. Replay historical buckets with measured local completion and human rates.
recommendation: WATCH and run a bounded, no-submit Fara-7B lab canary as the owned-hardware cost floor. Do not route production applications to it yet. Keep submit deterministic and permissioned, compare energy and fully loaded cost against premium agents, and reassess Fara1.5 only after official weights, harness, license, and ATS-specific evidence are available.
